column2: • Loss ratio benchmarks: NAIC Medical Professional Liability data, countrywide 2010–2024 (15-yr mean: 67.58%)
• Bot accuracy rates: PMC12047852 meta-analysis — Triage 97.8% | Diagnostic 61.4% | Treatment Recommendation 25.0%
• Harm rate: 15% of errors cause patient harm (range 3–15% per medical literature; conservative upper bound used)
• Base claim rate: 2% of harms result in a filed claim (Bureau of Justice Statistics)
• AI litigation multipliers (2x / 3x / 5x) are novel assumptions — no AI malpractice loss history exists
• Payout rate: 22% of filed claims result in payment (NPDB)
• Average severity: $350,000 per paid claim (NPDB $309,908 avg 2009–2018; Maryland 2023 ~$410,000)
• Trend slopes derived from linear regression 2018–2024: Favourable −0.414%/yr | Neutral 0% | Unfavourable +0.379%/yr
• Settlement lag: 2 years default — time from policy inception to full claim settlement
• LIMITATION: This is a demonstrative actuarial model only. No actual AI health bot malpractice loss data exists.
